NewLaunch.title: New Launch Menu
NewLaunch.leoOptimizedNote: Note: This tool is optimized for LEO satellites and may not work correctly for HEO or GEO orbits.

msgs.windowFound: Launch at {time} to match the target plane (ΔRAAN {delta}°)
msgs.timeRelativeToLaunch: Time is now relative to launch time.

errorMsgs.windowGeometryImpossible: No launch window: the target inclination is below the launch site latitude.
errorMsgs.invalidTle1Length: Invalid TLE1 Created: length is not 69 - {tle}
errorMsgs.invalidTle2Length: Invalid TLE2 Created: length is not 69 - {tle}
errorMsgs.failedAltitudeTest: Failed Altitude Test - Try a Different Satellite!
errorMsgs.cruncherFailed: Cruncher failed to meet requirement after multiple tries! Is this launch even possible?
errorMsgs.launchSiteNotInCatalog: Launch site {name} not found in launchSites catalog.
errorMsgs.eccentricityFormatIssue: There was an issue with this satellite's eccentricity format. Try a different satellite.
errorMsgs.epochYearFormatIssue: There was an issue with this satellite's epoch year format. Try a different satellite.
errorMsgs.invalidEpochDayFormat: Invalid epoch day format! Must be 3 digits, a decimal, and 8 digits after the decimal.
errorMsgs.epochDayFormatIssue: There was an issue with this satellite's epoch day format. Try a different satellite.
errorMsgs.failedToPropagate: Failed to propagate satellite. Try different parameters or report this issue if parameters are correct.

help.overview: The New Launch menu creates a notional launch: it copies a real satellite's orbit, rotates it so it lifts off from a launch site you choose, and resets the clock to T+0. Use it to answer "if something launches from this site into this kind of orbit, when will my sensors see it?"
help.imgAlt: The New Launch menu with launch facility and direction options
help.imgCaption: Pick a template satellite, a launch facility, and a northbound or southbound launch.
help.howToUse:
1. Select a satellite whose orbit resembles the expected payload; its inclination and altitude become the template. The icon is disabled until a satellite is selected.
  2. Open the menu and pick a Launch Facility (grouped by country) and whether the launch heads North or South.
  3. Click Create Launch Nominal. A new simulated object is created on the rotated orbit, and the clock resets to 00:00:00, so time is now relative to liftoff.
  4. Open sensor coverage menus (Look Angles, Multi-Site Looks) to see when the nominal first becomes visible after launch.
help.limits:
• The template's inclination must be at least the launch site's latitude: you cannot launch into an orbit tilted less than where you launch from, so the button disables itself in that case.
  • The tool is optimized for LEO; highly elliptical or GEO templates may fail.
  • The nominal is simulated: its position relative to other catalog objects is not meaningful.
  • Your selected template satellite is left unchanged; the nominal is a separate object in an analyst catalog slot.
help.tip1: After creation the clock reads time since liftoff: "00:45:00" means 45 minutes after launch, perfect for sensor-coverage timelines.
help.tip2: If the creation fails its altitude check, try a different template satellite with a more conventional orbit.

NewLaunch.launchWindowHelp: Finds the next launch time whose orbital plane matches the target satellite. This only sets the launch time; nothing is created until you press Create Launch Nominal.
